title: VisionClaw: The Open-Source AI Assistant That Sees What You See and Acts on Your Voice
author: Andy Nguyen
text:
Member-only story
VisionClaw: The Open-Source AI Assistant That Sees What You See and Acts on Your Voice
Imagine wearing smart glasses that don’t just record your life — they understand it, respond to it, and take action on your behalf.
The future of wearable AI isn’t coming. It’s already here, and it’s open-source.
Last week, I stumbled upon a GitHub repository that made me do a double-take. It’s called VisionClaw, and it might just be the most exciting project in the wearable AI space right now.
Why? Because it transforms ordinary Meta Ray-Ban smart glasses into a real-time, voice-and-vision AI assistant that can see what you see, hear what you say, and — here’s the kicker — actually do things for you.
What Exactly Is VisionClaw?
VisionClaw is an open-source iOS app that connects your Meta Ray-Ban smart glasses to Google’s Gemini Live API. But it’s more than just a voice assistant with eyes.
Think of it as Jarvis for your face.
When you put on your glasses and tap the AI button, here’s what becomes possible:
- “What am I looking at?” — Gemini sees through your glasses camera and describes the scene in real-time
- “Add milk to my shopping list” — It actually adds it to your connected…
